name: Fuel
description: A vehicle consumes a stored resource while driving, boosting, idling, or powering systems, and changes behavior when depleted.
design_notes: Fuel is a vehicle resource transaction, not just a UI bar. The design should define fuel_tank_id capacity, consumption sources, refill transaction rules, empty behavior, save persistence, and exploit prevention around refuel items, stations, and multiplayer ownership.
design_purpose: Make vehicle travel, route planning, and risk management depend on preparation.
Connect vehicles to economy, survival, repair, and upgrade systems.
Create meaningful decisions about idling, boosting, detours, and refueling stops.
player_fantasy: The player feels the freedom of vehicle travel while respecting the need to keep it supplied.

parameters.name: fuel_tank_id
parameters.description: Tank or energy store identifier attached to a vehicle instance.

parameters.name: fuel_capacity_liters
parameters.description: Maximum stored fuel or charge before upgrade and damage modifiers.

parameters.name: fuel_consumption_per_km
parameters.description: Travel-based consumption used for driving route cost and UI estimates.

parameters.name: idle_consumption_per_minute
parameters.description: Drain while the engine or powered systems run without meaningful travel.

parameters.name: refill_transaction_id
parameters.description: Idempotent transaction identifier for fuel items, stations, or shared resource spends.

parameters.name: empty_behavior_id
parameters.description: Behavior applied when usable fuel reaches depletion.

edge_cases: The driver exits or disconnects during a refuel transaction.
Fuel reaches zero while the vehicle is airborne, on a slope, or crossing a checkpoint.
A damaged fuel tank leaks while UI range estimates still assume normal capacity.
Two players attempt to refuel the same shared vehicle with different items.
common_bugs: Fuel drains from render delta and depends on frame rate.
Refuel items are consumed twice after retrying a failed network request.
Save/load restores vehicle position but resets fuel to full or empty.
Empty behavior disables input but not boost, engine audio, or AI driving.
balancing_notes: Fuel pressure works best when refueling routes and fallback travel are readable.
Open-world fuel scarcity can create memorable planning, but it should not strand players without recovery options.
Boost or heavy vehicle upgrades can increase consumption to create tradeoffs.
accessibility_notes: Show fuel warnings with text, icons, and route estimates rather than a tiny gauge only.
Allow relaxed fuel consumption or assist settings in exploration-focused modes.

implementation_notes.unity: Store fuel_tank_id, current units, capacity, and empty_behavior_id on the vehicle instance. Refuel should reserve fuel item instances, validate capacity, mutate tank state, and commit once through an inventory or station transaction.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep fuel state in a vehicle data resource and advance consumption from physics or simulation ticks. UI should display projected range from the same consumption model used by movement.
implementation_notes.unreal: A replicated vehicle component can own fuel state and server-authoritative refill requests. Apply consumption from movement or engine state on the authority, and replicate warning thresholds to UMG.
implementation_notes.web: Represent fuel as simulation state keyed by vehicle_id and fuel_tank_id. Browser clients should not trust local refuel clicks; use refill_transaction_id to make retries idempotent.

relationships.reason: Fuel limits sustained acceleration and route range by adding a consumable travel resource.
Shops or stations can sell refills through validated purchase transactions.
Bases can host storage or refueling structures that make travel planning strategic.

example_games.title: Mad Max
example_games.note: Uses fuel and vehicle resource pressure in open-world traversal.

example_games.title: Days Gone
example_games.note: Uses motorcycle fuel as part of route planning and survival pressure.
